frameworks.name: attention reorientation network
frameworks.one_line: posterior parietal hub and early visual cortices serving spatial reattending

frameworks.name: multispectral neural oscillations
frameworks.one_line: theta, alpha, and gamma rhythms index validity effects during reorientation

frameworks.name: HD-tDCS polarity model
frameworks.one_line: anodal versus cathodal stimulation produces opposing modulations of the same circuit

core_question: Does high-definition tDCS over occipital cortex modulate attentional reorientation differently by polarity in the brain regions that serve reorientation?
key_assumptions: occipital cortex is an upstream node of the attention reorientation pathway
validity effects in a Posner task index attentional reallocation
HD-tDCS polarity can up- or down-regulate cortical excitability focally
manipulations: HD-tDCS polarity (anodal vs cathodal vs sham) over occipital cortex
cue validity in a modified Posner task during MEG
key_findings: behavioral validity effect present in all three stimulation conditions
cathodal HD-tDCS reduced theta validity effects in occipital cortex
cathodal HD-tDCS reduced alpha validity effects in left occipital and parietal cortex versus anodal
anodal occipital stimulation increased gamma amplitude in right occipital cortex versus cathodal and sham
interpretations: occipital HD-tDCS exerts a polarity-dependent influence on multispectral oscillations serving attentional reorientation
